top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The day's biggest story is diplomatic theatre at opposite ends of Asia: Marco Rubio touched down in Kolkata this evening for a Quad reset with India, while Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir landed in Tehran trying to broker an Iran deal — both visits aimed at calming a Trump foreign policy that is simultaneously massing options for military action against Cuba. Bolivia's siege of La Paz is entering its fourth week with four dead, and the UN issued a 'grave concern' rebuke over a new Taliban decree that effectively legalises child marriage. The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak — declared a global health emergency by WHO last Saturday — has now passed 600 suspected cases and 139 suspected deaths.

stories.headline: Rubio lands in India for Quad reset as Trump-Modi ties wobble
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in Kolkata on Saturday afternoon for a four-day visit — his first to India since taking office — ahead of the Quad foreign ministers' meeting in Delhi on May 26. Rubio's mission is to repair a relationship strained by tariffs and by Washington's warming ties with Pakistan, which is currently mediating US-Iran talks.

stories.headline: Trump openly floats military action against Cuba as Rubio writes off diplomacy
stories.summary: President Trump told reporters Thursday that 'it looks like I'll be the one' to intervene militarily in Cuba, a day after the US indicted former leader Raul Castro over the 1996 shoot-down of two civilian planes. Secretary of State Rubio said Friday a diplomatic resolution with Havana is unlikely. US Southern Command has begun drafting options ranging from targeted strikes to a full ground invasion.

stories.headline: Pakistan army chief Munir lands in Tehran as US-Iran talks 'narrow the gaps'
stories.summary: General Asim Munir arrived in Iran's capital on Thursday for mediation talks alongside US envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Iran said Washington's latest one-page memo has 'narrowed the gaps' — the most positive signal in two weeks — but enriched uranium and Strait of Hormuz tolls remain unresolved sticking points.

stories.headline: Bolivia's capital under siege as protests against President Paz enter fourth week
stories.summary: La Paz remains paralysed by union, peasant and miner blockades demanding President Rodrigo Paz resign less than six months into his term. With year-on-year inflation at 14% and food, fuel and hospital oxygen running short, four people have now died — three from blockaded hospital supply cut-offs and one in clashes with police.

stories.headline: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda surges past 600 suspected cases
stories.summary: The WHO-declared global health emergency in DR Congo's Ituri Province now spans roughly 600 suspected cases and 139 suspected deaths, with the virus spreading into M23-controlled territory — complicating the response. India-Africa summit postponed. There is no licensed vaccine for the Bundibugyo strain; case fatality in past outbreaks has run 30-50%.

stories.headline: UN sounds alarm on new Taliban decree that implies child marriage is legal
stories.summary: UNAMA on Thursday expressed 'grave concern' over Decree No. 18 published by Afghanistan's justice ministry, which sets out rules for marital separation and states that the silence of a girl who has reached puberty can be taken as consent to marriage. Men retain unilateral divorce rights; women must use complex judicial routes. The Taliban says the decree reflects Islamic law.

stories.headline: Ukraine drone strike at Snizhne kills 65 Russian cadets and academy chief
stories.summary: Ukraine's 1st Separate Centre of Unmanned Systems Forces used 11 attack drones carrying 100kg warheads to hit a Sever-Akhmat regiment drone-training compound in occupied Donetsk on the night of May 19-20. Kyiv says 65 cadets and the centre's head — a doctor of the Russian Academy of Rocket and Artillery Sciences — were killed. Russia has not confirmed.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Saturday's most-watched story is political: Senior Minister of State for Manpower and Health Koh Poh Koon told Parliament on May 22 he is stepping down from public office on June 1, ending a 13-year political career. The Tuas-Simba acquisition of M1 also collapsed on May 22 after IMDA conditions weren't met by the deadline, leaving the telco market in limbo. Weather-wise, NEA is forecasting thundery showers on most days for the rest of May with Sumatra squalls on a few pre-dawn mornings. MOH continues to monitor the DR Congo Ebola outbreak but has not raised travel restrictions.

stories.headline: Koh Poh Koon to step down from public office on June 1
stories.summary: Senior Minister of State for Manpower (since 2021) and Health (since 2025) Koh Poh Koon announced on May 22 he will resign from public office on June 1. A former colorectal surgeon turned PAP MP in 2015, Koh said he is returning to medicine. His departure adds to a series of mid-tier ministerial exits since GE2025.

stories.headline: S$1.43 billion Simba-M1 acquisition collapses after IMDA probe
stories.summary: Tuas Ltd announced on May 22 that Simba's S$1.43 billion acquisition of M1 has been terminated after several conditions imposed by the Infocomm Media Development Authority were not met by the May 21 deadline. The collapse leaves Singapore's mobile telco landscape unchanged and is a blow to Tuas's regional consolidation plans.

stories.headline: NEA: thundery showers on most days for the rest of May, Sumatra squalls possible
stories.summary: NEA's fortnightly outlook forecasts short-duration afternoon thundery showers on most days through the end of May, occasionally stretching into evening. Sumatra squalls could bring widespread thundery showers with gusty winds on a few pre-dawn mornings. Daytime highs are pegged at 33-34 degrees, with some days touching 35.

stories.headline: June BTO launch: 6,900 flats across Ang Mo Kio, Bishan, Bukit Merah, Sembawang, Woodlands
stories.summary: HDB confirmed roughly 6,900 flats across seven projects for the June 2026 BTO launch, with about half falling under the Plus or Prime categories — including high-demand Bishan, Bukit Merah and Ang Mo Kio projects subject to tighter resale rules. HDB expects to launch about 19,600 flats in total this year.

stories.headline: MOH maintains travel advisory; CDA tracking Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: The Ministry of Health refreshed its traveller health advisory on May 19 and the Communicable Diseases Agency continues to monitor the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda. No new travel restrictions have been announced. MOH still recommends standard precautions for travel to affected districts.

stories.headline: BTS announce four-night Singapore tour in December
stories.summary: Live Nation announced on May 22 that BTS will play Singapore on December 17, 19, 20 and 22, partnering with The Kallang Group. Tickets go on sale June 3 in what organisers called 'a landmark moment for fans across Southeast Asia'. The four-night run is one of the band's largest Southeast Asia stops in years.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: Across all three theatres the through-line is fragile, unravelling ceasefires. Ukraine is for the first time in nearly two years recovering more territory than Russia is taking — but Russia has compensated by launching record drone volleys, and Trump's brokered 72-hour truce died after barely two days. The US-Iran ceasefire is holding by a thread, with Pakistan-mediated talks 'narrowing the gaps' but Hormuz tolls and Iran's enriched uranium still unresolved. Israel-Lebanon's 'extended' truce is barely worthy of the name — daily Israeli strikes have continued and an underground weapons factory in the Beqaa was hit this week.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active - Russia losing territory, drone war escalating

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
Last time before mid-2026 that Ukraine took back more land in a month than Russia seized.
Russia registers a net loss of 69 sq mi over four weeks — first sustained reversal in almost two years.
Russia launches 8,000+ drones in a month — highest monthly total of the war.
Trump brokers a 72-hour US-mediated truce, calls it 'the beginning of the end'.
Truce expires; Zelenskyy reports Russia fired 200+ drones overnight.
Ukrainian drone strike at Snizhne, Donetsk kills 65 Sever-Akhmat drone cadets plus academy head.
Russian total combat losses since 2022 hit ~1.354M personnel per Ukrainian general staff.
253 combat engagements in the past 24 hours; fiercest fighting in the Pokrovsk sector.
US and Israel launch Operation Epic Fury against Iran; Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei killed.
Iran declares Strait of Hormuz 'closed' to shipping; tanker traffic falls ~70% within days.
US-Iran ceasefire agreed, also covering Israel.
US imposes counter-blockade on Strait of Hormuz.
Only 191 vessels cross Hormuz the entire month vs ~3,000 in a typical pre-war month.
US declares offensive phase 'over'; Iran says it will not let US control Hormuz.
Pakistan army chief Munir arrives in Tehran; Iran says US one-page memo has 'narrowed the gaps'.
Iran's negotiator warns of 'crushing, bitter' consequences if US 'foolishly' restarts war.
Israel begins its 2026 ground war in Lebanon against Hezbollah; 1M+ Lebanese eventually displaced.
Worst three-day stretch of the month — 32, 36 then 51 Lebanese killed in successive days of Israeli strikes.
UN 'Board of Peace' director Mladenov says Gaza ceasefire hinges on Hamas disarmament.
Israel kills Izz al-Din al-Haddad, leader of Hamas in Gaza, in a Rimal apartment strike.
Lebanon ceasefire formally extended for another 45 days.
Israeli strike on southern Lebanon kills at least 10 civilians, including three women and three children.
IDF hits an underground Hezbollah weapons factory in the Beqaa Valley.
Lebanese health ministry tally: 3,111 killed, 9,432 injured since March 2.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius Andes hantavirus story is in its tail phase — no new cases have been reported since the ECDC's May 21 update, the ship has been disembarked and disinfected in Rotterdam, and WHO maintains its 'low' public-health risk rating. Total stands at 11 cases (nine confirmed, two probable) across four countries, with three deaths. The story to watch is the long incubation period — up to six weeks — which means more former passengers could still test positive in their home countries through June.

stories.headline: ECDC May 21 update: no new cases, more still expected
stories.summary: The European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control's May 21 update reports no new cases or deaths since the prior update. Total remains 11 cases — nine confirmed, two probable. The ECDC notes further cases among former passengers and crew are still expected given Andes hantavirus's six-week incubation window.

stories.headline: MV Hondius disembarked and disinfected in Rotterdam
stories.summary: The MV Hondius arrived in Rotterdam on May 18 where all 23 remaining crew — drawn from four countries — were retested and disembarked into quarantine. A deceased passenger's body was removed for cremation. Full disinfection of the vessel is now underway before it returns to service.

stories.headline: WHO: Andes virus confirmed - the only hantavirus that spreads human-to-human
stories.summary: WHO confirmed on May 6 that the outbreak is caused by Andes virus, normally found only in the Andes mountains of Argentina and Chile. Andes is the only known hantavirus capable of person-to-person transmission, which is what made shipboard spread possible. WHO has shipped 2,500 diagnostic kits from Argentina to labs in five countries.

stories.headline: Public health risk remains 'low' globally, WHO says
stories.summary: WHO's most recent risk assessment maintains a 'low' public health risk rating for the wider population, noting Andes virus does not appear to be spreading in the general population off the ship. The CDC continues active surveillance on US disembarked passengers; no US-linked cases have been confirmed.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: This cycle's defining threat is a Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN authentication bypass scoring a maximum CVSS 10.0, exploited as a zero-day by a sophisticated actor (UAT-8616) and added to the CISA KEV with a May 17 federal patch deadline. A 'Copy Fail' Linux kernel privilege escalation that affects every major distro shipped since 2017 is also under active exploitation. Microsoft's May Patch Tuesday cleared 120 flaws (no zero-days), and Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 paid out $1.298M for 47 zero-days, with DEVCORE crowned Master of Pwn. The new worry is AI-assisted exploitation — Google says a hacker group tried to use AI to engineer a 2FA bypass for mass exploitation.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN auth bypass CVE-2026-20182 actively exploited - CVSS 10.0
stories.summary: Cisco has patched a Catalyst SD-WAN Controller authentication bypass exploited as a zero-day by sophisticated actor UAT-8616. Crafted peering requests let an attacker log in as a high-privileged internal account on both on-prem and cloud deployments. CISA added it to the KEV with a May 17 federal patch deadline.

stories.headline: 'Copy Fail' Linux kernel root escalation hits every major distro since 2017
stories.summary: CVE-2026-31431 is a local privilege escalation in the Linux kernel that affects Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Amazon Linux 2023, RHEL 10.1, SUSE 16, Debian, Fedora and Arch. A 732-byte Python script reliably escalates an unprivileged user to root. CISA added it to the KEV on May 1 with a May 15 federal patch deadline.

stories.headline: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday: 120 flaws, no zero-days
stories.summary: Microsoft fixed 120 vulnerabilities, including 17 rated Critical (14 RCE, 2 elevation-of-privilege, 1 information-disclosure). Watch CVE-2026-35421 (Windows GDI RCE via a malicious EMF file in Microsoft Paint), CVE-2026-40365 (SharePoint authenticated RCE) and CVE-2026-41096 (Windows DNS Client RCE via a crafted DNS response).

stories.headline: FortiClient EMS CVE-2026-35616 actively exploited; emergency patch released
stories.summary: Fortinet has issued an emergency patch for CVE-2026-35616, an improper access-control flaw in FortiClient EMS that lets unauthenticated attackers execute code or commands via crafted requests. The bug is being actively exploited in attacks; administrators should patch immediately.

stories.headline: BitLocker zero-day 'YellowKey' bypasses Windows full-disk encryption
stories.summary: An anonymous researcher disclosed YellowKey, a BitLocker bypass that functions as a backdoor present only in the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE). A companion flaw, GreenPlasma, escalates privilege via Windows Collaborative Translation Framework (CTFMON). A proof-of-concept has been released.

stories.headline: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 closes - $1.298M paid for 47 zero-days, DEVCORE crowned Master of Pwn
stories.summary: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 wrapped May 16 with $1,298,250 in payouts for 47 zero-days across browsers, enterprise apps, virtualization, cloud-native and the new LLM category. Notable exploits hit SharePoint, Exchange, Edge and Windows. DEVCORE topped the standings with $505,000 and 50.5 Master of Pwn points; STAR Labs SG took second.

stories.headline: Google: AI used in the wild for the first time to develop a 2FA-bypass zero-day
stories.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group says it likely thwarted a hacker group that weaponised an AI model to discover and exploit a 2FA-bypass zero-day in a popular open-source server administration tool, intended for a 'mass exploitation event'. The team called it the first publicly observed malicious AI-assisted vulnerability discovery in the wild.

stories.headline: MiniPlasma Windows 0-day escalates to SYSTEM on fully patched Win11
stories.summary: MiniPlasma revives a 2020 Windows cldflt.sys flaw to gain SYSTEM access on fully patched Windows 11 systems running the May 2026 updates. The exploit reliably spawns a command prompt with SYSTEM privileges; no public patch yet. Microsoft has been notified.
